aksmon_en.hlp_strings: Hardlock modules and HASP keys for a network can be connected to any computers in the network. They are managed there by the local HL-Server software or the HASP License Manager and are made available within the network.
permits centralized administration of the various HL-Server programs and the Hardlock modules they provide and/or the HASP License Manager and the HASP network keys. Refer to
for information on program settings for
The following options are available for
Checking the Properties of the HASP License Manager
Starting and Stopping HASP License Manager as a Service
help file for further information on
The following options are available for
Checking and Editing the Properties of HL-Servers in the Network
Checking, Adding and Removing Hardlock Modules
Collecting and Importing Information for a License Update
Installing, Starting, Stopping and Uninstalling the Local HL-Server Service
Refer to the following help topics for background information on
Questions and Answers for Hardlock in a Network

akes it possible to administer HASP keys on an NT workstation. You can use the

The following information about the selected server is displayed here:
Name of the computer on which HL-Server is running.
OS version and version of th
Active protocols. Here, you can specify which protocols should be used.
You can influence which protocols the HL-Server uses, for example in order to limit the group of users for certain Hardlock modules.
Do this by activating or deactivating the corresponding protocol options.
Refer to the following help topic for information about the possible protocols:
Maximum number of logins since HL-Server started

Make sure that the Hardlock module you want to add is connected to the selected computer. If necessary, check whether the license file with the ALF extension is located in the correct folder. Refer to the following help topic:
Specify the module address and select
The module is added to the server and displayed in the table.
Removal may make it impossible to continue running programs which are logged on to the Hardlock module, resulting in a loss of data.
In the table, select the Hardlock module which you wish to remove.
The Hardlock module is now no longer made available within the network by the HL-Server.
All logins are deleted when you remove a Hardlock module.
The statistics show the transferred data categorized according to the protocol used and the transfer direction, as well as faults which have occurred.
